1	---
2	name: blueprint-writer
3	description: Update one blueprint chapter to reflect strategy changes, fill missing definitions or theorems, or align prose with the current Lean structure. Plan-agent-dispatched; one writer per chapter. May spawn reference-retriever mid-session when drafting reveals a missing source.
4	write_domain: "blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex"
5	read_only: false
6	can_spawn: true
7	default_enabled: true
8	dispatcher_notes: |
9	  - Dispatch one writer per chapter that the blueprint-reviewer's
10	    checklist flags as incomplete, lacking proof detail, or missing
11	    multi-route coverage.
12	  - Each directive must be precise: strategy context (the slice that
13	    matters for this chapter), required definitions/theorems (with
14	    enough mathematical detail to formalize), references, and
15	    explicit out-of-scope items. Writers do NOT speculate beyond
16	    what the directive lists.
17	  - **Authorize the retriever in the writer's --write-domain.** If
18	    the chapter might need fresh source material, dispatch the
19	    writer with TWO globs:
20	      --write-domain 'blueprint/src/chapters/<chapter>.tex'
21	      --write-domain 'references/**'
22	    The writer itself only edits its assigned chapter (its prompt
23	    body enforces that), but the second glob authorizes a child
24	    reference-retriever if the writer discovers it needs one. Omit
25	    `references/**` only when you are confident the writer will not
26	    need new sources (e.g. purely cleanup edits).
27	  - If the strategy has multiple viable routes and one route's chapters
28	    are missing, dispatch a writer per route to bring all routes to
29	    parity before any prover work begins. Do not push provers onto a
30	    route whose blueprint coverage is incomplete.
31	  - After a significant writer round, re-dispatch blueprint-reviewer
32	    in the same iteration to confirm the updated blueprint is now
33	    sufficient — do not assume the writer fixed everything.
34	  - If a writer's report contains entries under "Strategy-modifying
35	    findings", STOP and update STRATEGY.md before any further Lean
36	    work this iter. The writer is telling you the prose surfaced a
37	    strategy-level issue.
38	  - If a writer's report includes child reference-retriever dispatches,
39	    skim the new `references/<slug>.md` files before using the
40	    writer's output — the writer relied on them, so should you.
41	---
42	
43	# Blueprint Writer
44	
45	You write or revise **one blueprint chapter** under plan-agent direction. You receive a precise directive naming the target chapter, the strategy context the chapter must reflect, the definitions/theorems that must be present, and the scope of changes you may make.
46	
47	## Your Job
48	
49	The plan agent has decided that a specific blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<slug>.tex`) needs to change — to reflect a strategic decision, fill a missing definition or theorem the project needs, or align the informal prose with the current Lean structure. Your directive tells you which chapter, what must be there, and what counts as out-of-scope.
50	
51	You only edit the **one chapter named in your directive**. Your declared `--write-domain` should reflect that (`blueprint/src/chapters/<slug>.tex`). You do NOT edit other chapters, `.lean` files, or `content.tex`. Cross-chapter inconsistencies you spot go in the report's "Notes for Plan Agent" section — you flag them, you don't fix them.
52	
53	## Directive Format
54	
55	```markdown
56	# Blueprint Writer Directive
57	
58	## Slug
59	<slug>
60	
61	## Target chapter
62	blueprint/src/chapters/<chapter-slug>.tex
63	
64	## Strategy context
65	<the relevant slice of STRATEGY.md the plan agent extracted for you — typically a paragraph or two describing what this chapter must support in the overall arc. You do NOT read STRATEGY.md yourself; the plan agent gives you only what is relevant.>
66	
67	## Required content
68	- Definition <name>: <informal description of what it must define, with the mathematical content the prover needs in order to formalize>
69	- Theorem <name>: <statement + intent of the proof sketch the prover should formalize>
70	- Proof sketch for <theorem>: <how detailed; what cross-references; whether to expand a step into sub-lemmas>
71	- ...
72	
73	## Out of scope
74	<things that look related but the plan agent does NOT want you to touch this round>
75	
76	## References
77	- <path/to/reference.md or arxiv ID>: <which sections are relevant>
78	- ...
79	
80	## Expected outcome
81	<what the chapter should look like after, in one paragraph>
82	```
83	
84	If a section is omitted from your directive, the plan agent decided you don't need it. Don't speculate beyond what was given.
85	
86	## Chapter format
87	
88	Each declaration block in a chapter looks like:
89	
90	```latex
91	\begin{theorem}[name_for_humans]
92	  \label{thm:some_label}
93	  \lean{namespace.theorem_name}
94	  \uses{def:related_definition, lem:supporting_lemma}
95	  Informal statement of the theorem, using standard mathematical notation.
96	\end{theorem}
97	
98	\begin{proof}
99	  \uses{thm:another_result}
100	  Step-by-step informal proof. Reference blueprint labels with \uses{...}
101	  so the dependency graph stays accurate.
102	\end{proof}
103	```
104	
105	Use `\definition`, `\lemma`, `\theorem`, `\proposition`, `\corollary` as appropriate. `\lean{...}` names the Lean declaration this block corresponds to. `\uses{...}` records cross-references; keep it accurate so `leanblueprint`'s dependency graph remains usable.
106	
107	## Rules
108	
109	### What you CAN do
110	- Add new declaration blocks (definitions, lemmas, theorems, propositions, corollaries) under direction.
111	- Expand or revise existing prose / proof sketches in your assigned chapter.
112	- Add `\uses{...}` cross-references.
113	- Adjust `\lean{...}` hints when the directive names a new Lean target.
114	
115	### What you MUST do
116	- **Keep the chapter valid LaTeX.** Don't leave dangling `\begin{...}` without matching `\end{...}`. Compile-checking is the plan agent's responsibility but you must not introduce syntax errors.
117	- **Stay within your chapter.** Your declared write-domain is one `*.tex` file. The Archon CLI rejects writes outside it.
118	- **Define non-standard macros in `blueprint/src/macros/common.tex`** before using them — but: that file is outside your write-domain, so you DON'T touch it. If the directive requires a new macro, you note in your report "needs macro `\foo`" and leave the LaTeX using the new command name; the plan agent adds the macro before next iter's typeset.
119	- **Use mathematical, not Lean-syntactic prose.** Describe the proof in the language of mathematics — definitions, set inclusions, ring maps, universal properties — not in Lean tactic syntax. The prover formalizes your math.
120	- **Document every change** in your report.
121	
122	### What you MUST NOT do
123	- **Do NOT add `\leanok` or `\mathlibok` markers.** Those are managed by the `sync_leanok` phase + the review agent — never by you.
124	- **Do NOT edit other chapters.** Even when you spot a related issue, flag it in "Notes for Plan Agent" instead of fixing it.
125	- **Do NOT edit `content.tex`** (the top-level blueprint file that `\input`s the chapters).
126	- **Do NOT edit `.lean` files** or any other state file.
127	- **Do NOT write Lean syntax** — keep the chapter mathematical, not syntactic.
128	- **Do NOT expand scope.** Stick to what the directive listed under "Required content".
129	
130	## Reading references
131	
132	You write mathematics, not literature criticism — your prose must be grounded in authoritative sources, not your training memory. Before composing any new declaration block:
133	
134	1. **Read `references/summary.md`** to see the index of sources the project already has. Sources directly relevant to your chapter are your first stop.
135	2. **Read every reference file your directive names** under its `## References` section. The plan agent named those because the chapter needs them.
136	3. **Read anything in `references/summary.md` your directive didn't name but that is clearly relevant** to your chapter (same area, same theorem, same construction).
137	
138	Do NOT write content from your training memory when you could ground it in a reference instead. When the references say one thing and your memory says another, trust the references.
139	
140	## Dispatching a reference-retriever (when the project's sources don't have what you need)
141	
142	If, while drafting, you discover that the chapter needs material the project's `references/` doesn't contain, **dispatch a `reference-retriever` mid-session** rather than guessing or papering over the gap.
143	
144	Conditions for dispatch:
145	
146	- The directive named a source (`Smith 2018`, an arXiv ID) that isn't in `references/` yet.
147	- The required content is in a known textbook (Hartshorne, Vakil, Stacks Project, etc.) but the project hasn't summarized the relevant chapter.
148	- You need a Mathlib-adjacent source (nLab, math overflow, Stacks Project) the directive didn't anticipate.
149	
150	Dispatch (Bash, foreground, in your write-domain only if it includes `references/**`):
151	
152	```
153	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
154	  --name reference-retriever \
155	  --slug <kebab-slug-for-the-source> \
156	  --directive-file .archon/logs/iter-NNN/<your-slug>/reference-retriever-<child-slug>-directive.md \
157	  --write-domain 'references/**'
158	```
159	
160	Write the directive file first; the directive format is documented in `.archon/subagents/reference-retriever.md`. The retriever returns when the new summary is on disk; **then resume writing**, citing the new reference.
161	
162	If your invocation's recorded write-domain does NOT include `references/**` (your `parent.write_domain` in `dispatch.jsonl`), the dispatch will be rejected. In that case, STOP writing the affected section, report the missing source in "Notes for Plan Agent", and finish the parts of the chapter you can still write. The plan agent will re-dispatch you next iter with the broader write-domain.
163	
164	## Workflow
165	
166	1. Read your directive completely.
167	2. **Read `references/summary.md`** and every reference your directive points at; also read sibling-chapter material that informs cross-references.
168	3. Read the target chapter currently on disk to see what's already there.
169	4. Plan the edits: which blocks to add, which to revise. Decide where each new block goes in the chapter's existing flow.
170	5. **If a needed source is missing from `references/`, dispatch a `reference-retriever`** (see above) and wait for it to return before drafting the affected sections.
171	6. Make the edits.
172	7. Verify the file is still valid LaTeX at a glance (no unmatched begin/end, balanced braces in `\label`/`\uses`/`\lean`).
173	8. Write your report.
174	
175	## Logging
176	
177	Write your report to `.archon/task_results/blueprint-writer-<slug>.md` (or the parent-aware path under `task_results/<parent-slug>/` when invoked nested — your invocation prompt names the exact path).
178	
179	```markdown
180	# Blueprint Writer Report
181	
182	## Slug
183	<slug>
184	
185	## Status
186	<COMPLETE | INCOMPLETE>
187	<If INCOMPLETE: which required items could not be written and why.>
188	
189	## Target chapter
190	blueprint/src/chapters/<chapter-slug>.tex
191	
192	## Changes Made
193	- **Added definition** `\definition`/`\label{def:foo}`/`\lean{Foo.bar}` — <one line on what it captures>
194	- **Added theorem** `\theorem`/`\label{thm:foo}` — <one line on statement>
195	  - Proof sketch added: <Y/N + brief shape>
196	- **Revised** `<existing label>` — <one line on what changed>
197	- ...
198	
199	## Cross-references introduced
200	- `\uses{thm:bar}` added in proof of `\thm:foo` — verify `thm:bar` exists in <chapter or this same one>
201	- ...
202	
203	## Macros needed (if any)
204	- `\foo{...}` — used in <where>; needs definition in `macros/common.tex`. NOT added by me (out of write-domain).
205	
206	## Reference-retriever dispatches (if any)
207	- slug `<child-slug>`: requested `<source>`. Status: COMPLETE / NOT_FOUND / PARTIAL. Summary at `references/<slug>.md`.
208	- ...
209	
210	## Notes for Plan Agent
211	- <any inconsistency I noticed in sibling chapters that the directive didn't cover>
212	- <any structural concern: e.g. "the chapter is now 800 lines, consider splitting">
213	- <any reference I had to guess at because the directive's reference section was incomplete>
214	
215	## Strategy-modifying findings
216	<populate this section ONLY if writing the chapter surfaced a need to
217	change the project's strategy itself — not just the chapter content.
218	Examples:
219	- A definition you were asked to write turns out to require a property
220	  that the strategy assumes is automatic but is not.
221	- A theorem you sketched is provable as stated, but its consequence
222	  used elsewhere in STRATEGY.md does not follow from it.
223	- A reference cited as authoritative actually requires a different
224	  setup than the strategy assumes.
225	
226	When this section is non-empty, the plan agent must update STRATEGY.md
227	before any further Lean work this iteration. Do NOT silently adapt the
228	chapter to paper over a strategy-level issue — surface it here.>
229	```
230	
231	## Return Value
232	
233	Your final assistant message:
234	
235	- One line: `<slug>: COMPLETE | INCOMPLETE — <one-sentence outcome>`
236	- The path to your full report.
237	
238	Keep the inline return short. The plan agent reads the full report.

1	\chapter{The symmetric monoidal category of $\mathcal O_X$-modules}
2	\label{chap:Modules_Monoidal}
3	
4	This chapter opens Phase~C by introducing the symmetric monoidal structure on the category of $\mathcal O_X$-modules for a scheme $X$. It is a prerequisite for Chapter~\ref{chap:Picard_LineBundle}: the definition of a \emph{line bundle} as an invertible object requires a monoidal category to invert in. Mathlib provides the structure on the presheaf side (\texttt{PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory}) and the sheafification machinery (\texttt{PresheafOfModules.sheafification}); the gap addressed here is the transfer of the monoidal structure across sheafification to the sheaf side, yielding an instance \texttt{MonoidalCategory $X$.Modules}.
5	
6	The chapter introduces no protected declarations: every definition and lemma here is auxiliary, supplying the Mathlib-gap-fill that unblocks the refinement of $\mathrm{LineBundle}$ from a global-sections approximation to the geometric definition.
7	
8	\section{Setup}
9	
10	Fix a scheme $X$. Recall (Chapter~\ref{chap:Cohomology_StructureSheafAb}) that $\mathcal O_X$-modules are organised as a category $X.\mathrm{Modules}$, packaged as $\mathrm{SheafOfModules}(\mathcal O_X)$ where $\mathcal O_X$ is regarded as a sheaf of (commutative) rings on the underlying topological space of $X$. The presheaf-of-modules variant $\mathrm{PresheafOfModules}(\mathcal O_X)$ already carries a symmetric monoidal structure in Mathlib (\texttt{PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory}), with tensor product computed pointwise on each open: for two presheaves of modules $M, N$,
11	\[
12	  (M \otimes_{\mathcal O_X} N)(U) \;=\; M(U) \otimes_{\mathcal O_X(U)} N(U).
13	\]
14	The associator, unitors, and braiding are inherited pointwise from $\mathrm{ModuleCat}\,\mathcal O_X(U)$.
15	
16	\section{Tensor product of sheaves of modules}
17	
18	\begin{definition}\leanok
19	[Tensor product of $\mathcal O_X$-modules]
20	  \label{def:Modules_tensorObj}
21	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj}
22	  Let $X$ be a scheme and $M, N \in X.\mathrm{Modules}$. The \emph{tensor product} $M \otimes_{\mathcal O_X} N$ is defined as the sheafification of the presheaf tensor product:
23	  \[
24	    M \otimes_{\mathcal O_X} N \;:=\; \mathrm{sheafify}\bigl(M_{\mathrm{psh}} \otimes_{\mathcal O_X, \mathrm{psh}} N_{\mathrm{psh}}\bigr),
25	  \]
26	  where $(-)_{\mathrm{psh}}$ is the forgetful functor $X.\mathrm{Modules} \to \mathrm{PresheafOfModules}\,\mathcal O_X$, and the inner tensor product is the pointwise one supplied by Mathlib.
27	\end{definition}
28	
29	\begin{remark}
30	  When $X$ is affine, both factors are quasi-coherent and the presheaf tensor product is already a sheaf on the basis of basic opens, so the sheafification step collapses to an isomorphism; but for a general scheme $X$ (and a general pair of $\mathcal O_X$-modules) the presheaf tensor product is genuinely not a sheaf and sheafification is essential.
31	\end{remark}
32	
33	\begin{theorem}\leanok
34	[Symmetric monoidal structure on $X.\mathrm{Modules}$]
35	  \label{thm:Modules_MonoidalCategory}
36	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.instMonoidalCategory}
37	  \uses{def:Modules_tensorObj}
38	  For every scheme $X$, the category $X.\mathrm{Modules}$ carries a canonical symmetric monoidal structure with tensor product given by Definition~\ref{def:Modules_tensorObj} and tensor unit $\mathcal O_X$ itself (as the trivial $\mathcal O_X$-module).
39	\end{theorem}
40	
41	\begin{proof}
42	  \uses{def:Modules_tensorObj}
43	  \leanok
44	  Set $L := \mathrm{sheafification}$, regarded as the left adjoint $\mathrm{PresheafOfModules}\,\mathcal O_X \to X.\mathrm{Modules}$, and let $F$ denote its right adjoint (the forgetful functor). The right adjoint $F$ is fully faithful, so by general localisation theory the localisation $L \dashv F$ realises $X.\mathrm{Modules}$ as the localisation of $\mathrm{PresheafOfModules}\,\mathcal O_X$ at the class $W := L^{-1}(\mathrm{Iso})$ of morphisms inverted by sheafification.
45	
46	  We claim $W$ is \emph{monoidal} in the sense of \cite{CategoryTheory.LocalizedMonoidal}: it is stable under left and right whiskering by any presheaf of modules. Indeed, if $L(f)$ is an iso, then for any presheaf of modules $X_0$,
47	  \[
48	    L(X_0 \,\triangleleft\, f) \;=\; L(X_0) \,\triangleleft\, L(f),
49	  \]
50	  since sheafification is a left adjoint and hence preserves colimits, and the presheaf tensor bifunctor preserves colimits in each variable (the pointwise tensor is computed in $\mathrm{ModuleCat}\,\mathcal O_X(U)$, which is cocomplete). The right-hand side is an iso because $L(f)$ is. Symmetrically for right whiskering.
51	
52	  With $W$ monoidal, the standard \texttt{LocalizedMonoidal} construction equips the localisation $X.\mathrm{Modules} = L(\mathrm{PresheafOfModules}\,\mathcal O_X)/W$ with a unique symmetric monoidal structure making $L$ a symmetric monoidal functor. The tensor product on the sheaf side is computed as $L$ applied to the presheaf-side tensor, which matches Definition~\ref{def:Modules_tensorObj}. The associator/unitors/braiding are images of their presheaf counterparts under $L$, and the pentagon/triangle/hexagon coherence axioms transfer by functoriality of $L$ and the universal property of localisation.
53	\end{proof}
54	
55	\begin{remark}[Mathlib precedent]
56	  Mathlib has the same pattern at one level of abstraction higher: \texttt{CategoryTheory.Sheaf.monoidalCategory} lifts a symmetric monoidal structure from a value category $A$ to $\mathrm{Sheaf}\,J\,A$ provided $J.W$ is monoidal and $A$ has weak sheafification ($J.W$-isomorphisms are detected pointwise after sheafification). The condensed case (\texttt{LightCondensed.instMonoidalCategorySheafLightProfiniteCoherentTopologyModuleCat}) shows this pattern in action. The wrinkle in our situation is that the value category varies with the open set (we have a sheaf of rings, not a fixed ring), so the generic instance \texttt{Sheaf.monoidalCategory} does not apply directly --- instead we adapt the parallel \texttt{LocalizedMonoidal} pattern to the presheaf-of-modules formalism by establishing $W.\texttt{IsMonoidal}$ for $W := L^{-1}(\mathrm{Iso})$. The proof is bookkeeping rather than mathematics: colimit-preservation of left adjoints applied to the (separately colimit-preserving) presheaf-tensor bifunctor.
57	\end{remark}
58	
59	\begin{remark}[Status of $W.\mathrm{IsMonoidal}$ and the stalks-level argument]
60	  Mathematically, $W.\mathrm{IsMonoidal}$ is the statement: \emph{if $L(f)$ is an isomorphism in $X.\mathrm{Modules}$, then so is $L(g \triangleleft f)$ for any presheaf of modules $g$} (and symmetrically for right whiskering). Concretely, $L(f)$ is iso iff $f$ becomes iso on each stalk (since sheafification is local), so we must show that $g \triangleleft f$ is stalkwise iso. At the stalk $x$, the presheaf tensor product computes as the $\mathcal O_{X,x}$-balanced tensor product $g_x \otimes_{\mathcal O_{X,x}} f_x$; tensoring with the identity on $g_x$ preserves stalk-level isomorphisms (since base change along a flat module factor preserves isos). This is the stalks-level argument the Lean side is attempting (iter-080 Lane 3). The bookkeeping required is non-trivial because Mathlib does not yet expose the stalk-of-presheaf-tensor identification in the varying-ring case; the alternative routes (closedness via internal hom; \texttt{sheafificationCompToSheaf} to \texttt{AddCommGrpCat}-side $W.\mathrm{IsMonoidal}$) are both blocked because \texttt{MonoidalClosed} for \texttt{PresheafOfModules}\,$R_0$ is absent for varying $R_0$ and \texttt{toPresheaf}\,$R_0$ is not monoidal (it sends a ring-balanced tensor to a $\mathbb Z$-tensor, which differs). The gap-fill remains the only outstanding piece of Phase~C step~C0; it does \emph{not} block downstream consumers, which use $\mathrm{MonoidalCategory}\,X.\mathrm{Modules}$ directly rather than $W.\mathrm{IsMonoidal}$ as a hypothesis.
61	\end{remark}
62	
63	\section{Invertible objects and line bundles}
64	
65	\begin{definition}[Invertible $\mathcal O_X$-module]
66	  \label{def:Modules_Invertible}
67	  An $\mathcal O_X$-module $L$ is \emph{invertible} (or a \emph{line bundle}) if there exists $L' \in X.\mathrm{Modules}$ and an isomorphism $L \otimes_{\mathcal O_X} L' \cong \mathcal O_X$.
68	\end{definition}
69	
70	Equivalently, $L$ is invertible iff it is an invertible object of the symmetric monoidal category $X.\mathrm{Modules}$ in the sense of \texttt{CategoryTheory.MonoidalCategory.Invertible}.
71	
72	This is the substrate that Chapter~\ref{chap:Picard_LineBundle} is supposed to use to define $\mathrm{LineBundle}\,X$. The current chapter (still using the global-sections approximation $\mathrm{CommRing.Pic}\,\Gamma(X, \top)$) is forward-compatible with this refinement; the refinement is the content of Phase~C step~C1 (see \texttt{STRATEGY.md}).
73	
74	\section{Mathlib gap}
75	
76	Mathlib provides:
77	\begin{itemize}
78	  \item \texttt{PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory} on the presheaf side (\texttt{Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Presheaf/Monoidal.lean}).
79	  \item \texttt{PresheafOfModules.sheafification} (\texttt{Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Presheaf/Sheafification.lean}) and \texttt{SheafOfModules.toSheafCompSheafToPresheafIso} as the comparison data.
80	  \item The generic \texttt{CategoryTheory.Sheaf.monoidalCategory} pattern for fixed-value monoidal categories.
81	\end{itemize}
82	
83	What is \emph{missing} and supplied by this chapter:
84	\begin{itemize}
85	  \item The instance \texttt{MonoidalCategory $X$.Modules} for $X$ a scheme. This is bookkeeping work over the existing presheaf-side instance.
86	  \item The invertibility predicate (Definition~\ref{def:Modules_Invertible}) and its API.
87	\end{itemize}
88	
89	\section{Use in the project}
90	
91	Theorem~\ref{thm:Modules_MonoidalCategory} unblocks:
92	\begin{itemize}
93	  \item Chapter~\ref{chap:Picard_LineBundle}, step~C1 of Phase~C: redefining $\mathrm{LineBundle}\,X$ as the type of invertible $\mathcal O_X$-modules (instead of the global-sections approximation).
94	  \item Chapter~\ref{chap:Picard_Functor}, step~C2: the relative Picard functor reformulated on the corrected $\mathrm{LineBundle}$.
95	  \item Chapter~\ref{chap:Picard_Functor}, step~C3: the representability theorem (FGA-style or via symmetric powers) is honestly stateable only after this refinement.
96	\end{itemize}
97	
98	\section{Formalization status}
99	
100	The Lean-side scaffolding lives in \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Modules/Monoidal.lean} and carries the three signatures \texttt{Scheme.Modules.tensorObj}, \texttt{Scheme.Modules.instMonoidalCategoryStruct}, and \texttt{Scheme.Modules.instMonoidalCategory}. The bodies are filled across successive prover rounds: the file is the active target of Phase~C step~C0 in the project strategy.
101

6	import Mathlib
7	
8	/-!
9	# The symmetric monoidal structure on `X.Modules`
10	
11	Phase C step C0 (per `STRATEGY.md`): the category of $\mathcal O_X$-modules
12	on a scheme $X$ carries a canonical symmetric monoidal structure, with
13	tensor product given by sheafification of the presheaf tensor product and
14	tensor unit given by the structure sheaf $\mathcal O_X$ itself.
15	
16	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Modules_Monoidal.tex`.
17	
18	## Status (iteration 077 — refactor-subagent scaffold)
19	
20	This file scaffolds the three signatures (`tensorObj`,
21	`instMonoidalCategoryStruct`, `instMonoidalCategory`) needed by Phase C
22	step C1 (refining `LineBundle` from `CommRing.Pic Γ(X, ⊤)` to the
23	invertible-object definition). The bodies are `sorry` for the prover
24	rounds iter-078+ to fill, using:
25	
26	- `PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory` (presheaf-side structure)
27	- `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` (pointwise tensor)
28	- `PresheafOfModules.sheafification` (left adjoint to inclusion)
29	- `SheafOfModules.toSheafCompSheafToPresheafIso` (the comparison data)
30	
31	## Mathematical content
32	
33	For two $\mathcal O_X$-modules $M$ and $N$,
34	$$
35	  M \otimes_{\mathcal O_X} N
36	    := \mathrm{sheafify}(M_{\mathrm{psh}} \otimes_{\mathcal O_X, \mathrm{psh}} N_{\mathrm{psh}}).
37	$$
38	The associator/unitors/braiding on the sheaf side are sheafifications of their
39	presheaf counterparts; the coherence axioms transfer via the universal
40	property of sheafification.
41	-/
42	
43	set_option autoImplicit false
44	
45	universe u
46	
47	open CategoryTheory MonoidalCategory
48	
49	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules
50	
51	variable (X : Scheme.{u})
52	
53	/-- The tensor product of two $\mathcal O_X$-modules: sheafify the presheaf
54	tensor product of the underlying presheaves-of-modules.
55	
56	The instance lookup for `MonoidalCategoryStruct (PresheafOfModules R₀)` requires
57	`R₀` to syntactically present as `R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat`. Since
58	`X.ringCatSheaf.obj = X.sheaf.val ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat` only
59	definitionally (through `sheafCompose`), we use `show` to expose the form. -/
60	noncomputable def tensorObj (M N : X.Modules) : X.Modules :=
61	  letI : MonoidalCategoryStruct (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf.obj) :=
62	    show MonoidalCategoryStruct
63	      (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (X.sheaf.obj ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat.{u} RingCat.{u})) from
64	      inferInstance
65	  (_root_.PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).obj
66	    ((Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).obj M ⊗ (Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).obj N)
67	
68	/-- Bridging instance: `PresheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf.obj` carries the
69	`MonoidalCategory` structure inherited from `PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory`
70	through the definitional equality `X.ringCatSheaf.obj = X.sheaf.obj ⋙ forget₂ ...`.
71	Needed for `MorphismProperty.IsMonoidal`, `LocalizedMonoidal`, and all downstream
72	constructions in this file to typecheck. -/
73	noncomputable instance instMonoidalCategoryPresheaf :
74	    MonoidalCategory (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf.obj) :=
75	  show MonoidalCategory
76	    (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (X.sheaf.obj ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat.{u} RingCat.{u})) from
77	    inferInstance
78	
79	/-- The presheaf-to-sheaf sheafification functor for `X.Modules`. Abbreviated `L`
80	in comments. -/
81	noncomputable abbrev sheafificationFunctor :
82	    _root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⥤ X.Modules :=
83	  _root_.PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)
84	
85	/-- The morphism property in `PresheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf.obj` consisting of
86	morphisms inverted by sheafification. By `Adjunction.isLocalization` together with
87	fullness/faithfulness of the right adjoint of `PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction`,
88	the sheafification functor is a localization at this class. -/
89	noncomputable abbrev W : MorphismProperty
90	    (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf.obj) :=
91	  (MorphismProperty.isomorphisms _).inverseImage (sheafificationFunctor X)
92	
93	/-- Sheafification is a localization at `W`. Follows from `Adjunction.isLocalization`
94	since the right adjoint of `sheafification (𝟙 _)` is Full + Faithful. -/
95	noncomputable instance sheafificationIsLocalization :
96	    (sheafificationFunctor X).IsLocalization (W X) :=
97	  (_root_.PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction
98	    (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).isLocalization
99	
100	/-- GAP-FILL: sheafification of `PresheafOfModules` sends presheaf-tensor whiskering
101	of an iso-inverted morphism to an iso. This is the single semantic gap-fill
102	needed to invoke the `LocalizedMonoidal` machinery (`CategoryTheory.Localization.Monoidal`)
103	on the sheafification functor.
104	
105	Mathematical content. The class `W X` of morphisms in `PresheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf.obj`
106	inverted by sheafification is stable under left- and right-whiskering by any presheaf-of-modules.
107	This holds because sheafification is a left adjoint to a fully-faithful right adjoint, and the
108	presheaf tensor product `tensorLeft`/`tensorRight` are colimit-preserving bifunctors on each
109	variable (see `PresheafOfModules.tensorLeft.PreservesColimitsOfSize` in
110	`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Presheaf/Monoidal.lean:237,243`).
111	
112	Closing this `IsMonoidal` instance below the level of `MorphismProperty.IsMonoidal.mk'` reduces
113	to the iso-stability of `sheafification` under presheaf-tensor whiskering, which is the analog
114	for `PresheafOfModules` of `GrothendieckTopology.W.monoidal` for fixed-value sheaves
115	(`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/Monoidal.lean:149`). The latter is proved via internal-hom
116	adjunction; the `PresheafOfModules` version needs the same technique but adapted to the
117	varying-value-category setting — substantial Mathlib infrastructure not currently present.
118	
119	## Iter-080 investigation (stalks-level route).
120	
121	The plan-agent recipe for this iteration was to attempt closure via stalks. After
122	`simp only [MorphismProperty.inverseImage_iff, MorphismProperty.isomorphisms.iff] at hf hg ⊢`
123	the goal reduces to `IsIso (L (f ⊗ₘ g))` with `hf : IsIso (L f)` and `hg : IsIso (L g)`,
124	where `L = sheafificationFunctor X`. Applying `MonoidalCategory.tensorHom_def` decomposes
125	`f ⊗ₘ g = (f ▷ Y₁) ≫ (X₂ ◁ g)`, so `L (f ⊗ₘ g) = L (f ▷ Y₁) ≫ L (X₂ ◁ g)` via
126	`Functor.map_comp`. The remaining content is `IsIso (L (f ▷ Y₁))` and `IsIso (L (X₂ ◁ g))`
127	— sheafification preserves whisker-iso.
128	
129	Mathematically, stalkwise, `(f ▷ Y₁)_x = f_x ⊗_{R₀.stalk x} id_{(Y₁)_x}` in `ModuleCat`
130	over the stalk ring. Tensoring with the identity preserves isos in any monoidal category.
131	Combined with `WEqualsLocallyBijective` (an iso after sheafification ⟺ locally bijective
132	⟺ stalkwise iso), this gives `IsIso (L (f ▷ Y₁))` from `IsIso (L f)`.
133	
134	The blocker is the precise Mathlib identification:
135	`(M ⊗_psh N).stalk x ≅ M.stalk x ⊗_{R₀.stalk x} N.stalk x`
136	(stalks-of-presheaf-tensor = tensor-of-stalks, over the stalk ring, in the varying-ring
137	setting). Mathlib has the analogous statement for fixed-ring `ModuleCat R` (see
138	`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Tilde.lean`), and stalks-commute-with-colimits is
139	abstractly available, but the explicit identification for `PresheafOfModules R₀` with
140	varying `R₀` is absent from `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Presheaf/Sheafify.lean`
141	and the surrounding modules — searches via `lean_leansearch` for "stalk presheaf tensor"
142	and grep for "stalk.*tensor" in `Mathlib/Algebra/Category` return nothing.
143	
144	Alternative routes investigated and ruled out (preserved from iter-079):
145	- `tensorHom_def` decomposition alone is circular at the abstract level — it reduces to
146	  the same whiskerLeft/whiskerRight closure question.
147	- `sheafificationCompToSheaf` (sheafification of `PresheafOfModules` = sheafification of
148	  underlying abelian-grp presheaves) gives an iso-detection in `AddCommGrpCat`, but the
149	  tensor products on the two sides do not commute (`PresheafOfModules` tensor is
150	  $R_0(U)$-balanced, `AddCommGrpCat` tensor is $\mathbb{Z}$-balanced), so the iso-stability
151	  question on the abelian-grp side does not transfer to the module side under tensor.
152	- Closedness via internal hom requires `MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R₀)`, which
153	  Mathlib provides only for fixed-ring `ModuleCat R`
154	  (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Monoidal.Closed`), not for varying-ring
155	  `PresheafOfModules R₀`. A multi-PR Mathlib upstream effort would be needed.
156	
157	Marked sorry until the upstream gap (stalk-of-presheaf-tensor in the varying-ring setting)
158	is filled. Per user policy 2026-05-11, no project-local helper lemma may be introduced to
159	bridge it — the closure must remain Mathlib-only.
160	
161	Note: this sorry does NOT block downstream consumers, which use `MonoidalCategory X.Modules`
162	(set up by `instMonoidalCategoryStruct` + `instMonoidalCategory` below, both fully closed
163	iter-079 via `LocalizedMonoidal`). Only consumers that take `(W X).IsMonoidal` as an
164	explicit hypothesis would be affected, and there are none in the project. Phase C step C1
165	(LineBundle refinement) is unblocked. -/
166	noncomputable instance instIsMonoidal_W : (W X).IsMonoidal := by
167	  -- `IsMonoidal.mk'` reduces to: if `L f` and `L g` are isos in `X.Modules`,
168	  -- then `L (f ⊗ₘ g)` is also iso. Substantive content: iso-stability of
169	  -- sheafification under presheaf-tensor whiskering. See docstring.
170	  refine MorphismProperty.IsMonoidal.mk' _ fun {X₁ X₂ Y₁ Y₂} f g hf hg => ?_
171	  simp only [MorphismProperty.inverseImage_iff,
172	    MorphismProperty.isomorphisms.iff] at hf hg ⊢
173	  sorry
174	
175	/-- The symmetric monoidal category structure on `X.Modules`: derived from the
176	`LocalizedMonoidal` machinery applied to the sheafification functor
177	`L : PresheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⥤ X.Modules` at the class `W X` of morphisms
178	inverted by `L`, with unit `L.obj (𝟙_ presheaf)` and `ε := Iso.refl _`.
179	
180	The localization's `tensorObj` is `((tensorBifunctor L W ε).obj M).obj N`, which is
181	naturally isomorphic to (but not definitionally equal to) the `tensorObj` defined above
182	as `L.obj (F.obj M ⊗ F.obj N)`; both unfold to sheafification of the presheaf tensor. -/
183	noncomputable instance instMonoidalCategoryStruct :
184	    MonoidalCategoryStruct (X.Modules) :=
185	  inferInstanceAs (MonoidalCategoryStruct
186	    (LocalizedMonoidal (sheafificationFunctor X) (W X) (Iso.refl _)))
187	
188	/-- The pentagon, triangle, and hexagon axioms transfer from the presheaf side
189	through the localization-monoidal universal property. -/
190	noncomputable instance instMonoidalCategory :
191	    MonoidalCategory (X.Modules) :=
192	  inferInstanceAs (MonoidalCategory
193	    (LocalizedMonoidal (sheafificationFunctor X) (W X) (Iso.refl _)))
194	
195	/-- Bridging instance exposing the symmetric (hence braided) structure on the
196	presheaf side `PresheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf.obj`, mirroring
197	`instMonoidalCategoryPresheaf` above. Needed for `Localization.Monoidal.Braided`
198	to derive `BraidedCategory (X.Modules)` through the sheafification localization. -/
199	noncomputable instance instBraidedCategoryPresheaf :
200	    BraidedCategory (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf.obj) :=
201	  show BraidedCategory
202	    (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (X.sheaf.obj ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat.{u} RingCat.{u})) from
203	    inferInstance
204	
205	/-- The braided (symmetric) structure on `X.Modules`, transported from the
206	presheaf side through the `LocalizedMonoidal` machinery. Needed by Phase C step
207	C1 (`Picard.LineBundle`) to expose `CommMonoid (Skeleton X.Modules)` and hence
208	`CommGroup (Skeleton X.Modules)ˣ`. -/
209	noncomputable instance instBraidedCategory : BraidedCategory (X.Modules) :=
210	  inferInstanceAs (BraidedCategory
211	    (LocalizedMonoidal (sheafificationFunctor X) (W X) (Iso.refl _)))
212	
213	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules

1	# Analogy: Mathlib idiom for the Picard group of a scheme as units of a monoidal-category skeleton
2	
3	## Slug
4	c1-route
5	
6	## Iteration
7	108
8	
9	## Question
10	
11	What is the canonical Mathlib idiom for defining the **Picard group of a
12	scheme as the units of a monoidal category of `O_X`-modules**, and is that
13	idiom executable today against the project's already-shipped
14	`MonoidalCategory (X.Modules)` / `BraidedCategory (X.Modules)` chain
15	(which transitively depends on the deferred `instIsMonoidal_W` sorry for
16	`(W X).IsMonoidal`)?
17	
18	## Project artifact(s)
19	
20	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/LineBundle.lean:85-122` — current
21	  `LineBundle X := CommRing.Pic Γ(X, ⊤)` and `Pic.pullback` via
22	  `CommRing.Pic.mapRingHom`.
23	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Modules/Monoidal.lean:166-194` — the deferred
24	  `instIsMonoidal_W` sorry plus the `instMonoidalCategoryStruct` and
25	  `instMonoidalCategory` instances that transitively consume it.
26	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/Functor.lean:108-174` — `quotMap` and
27	  `PicardFunctor`, parametric in `Pic.pullback`.
28	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FunctorAb.lean` — additive-group flavor
29	  of the same construction.
30	
31	## Decisions identified
32	
33	The directive's single design question compresses four orthogonal decisions.
34	
35	### Decision A: name of the "invertible-objects" type
36	
37	- **Mathlib idiom**: there is **no type literally named
38	  `MonoidalCategory.Invertible`** in Mathlib b80f227. The canonical idiom is
39	  `(Skeleton C)ˣ` — the **units of the (skeleton's) monoid structure on the
40	  objects of a (braided) monoidal category**. Specifically:
41	  - `CategoryTheory.Skeleton.instMonoid` (`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Skeleton:60`)
42	    promotes `Skeleton C` to `Monoid` from `[MonoidalCategory C]`.
43	  - `CategoryTheory.Skeleton.instCommMonoid` (`...:80`) promotes it to
44	    `CommMonoid` from `[BraidedCategory C]`.
45	  - `(Skeleton C)ˣ` is then automatically `CommGroup` from `instCommGroupUnits`.
46	  - The ring precedent (`CommRing.Pic`,
47	    `Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup:407-408`) defines
48	    `def CommRing.Pic (R) := Shrink (Skeleton (SemimoduleCat R))ˣ`.
49	
50	  Why Mathlib chose this: skeleton-level units neatly factor the
51	  *non-categorical* equational content (groupy laws on iso-classes) from
52	  the *categorical* content (associator/unitor/braiding witnesses). The
53	  `Shrink` wrapper handles universe issues (`SemimoduleCat R`'s skeleton
54	  lives in `u+1` a priori; Mathlib proves it is `Small.{u}` via the
55	  finite-presentation classifier).
56	
57	- **Project's proposed path** (`STRATEGY.md` "Phase C1" §): refactor
58	  `LineBundle X` to "`MonoidalCategory.Invertible (X.Modules)`". This name
59	  **does not denote any Mathlib declaration**; it is a placeholder that
60	  must be resolved to the actual idiom.
61	
62	- **Gap**: divergent-and-wrong (a literal `MonoidalCategory.Invertible`
63	  does not exist; the strategy text needs to commit to the correct name).
64	  Easy to fix at refactor-spec time.
65	
66	- **Verdict**: **ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB**. Refactor target should be
67	  `(Skeleton X.Modules)ˣ` (or `Shrink (Skeleton X.Modules)ˣ` if a
68	  universe-size issue arises), mirroring `CommRing.Pic`.
69	
70	### Decision B: dependency on the deferred `instIsMonoidal_W` sorry
71	
72	- **Mathlib idiom**: `(Skeleton C)ˣ` as a `CommGroup` requires
73	  `[BraidedCategory C]` (to get `CommMonoid (Skeleton C)`).
74	  - For the project, `BraidedCategory (X.Modules)` is obtained transitively
75	    via `Localization.Monoidal.Braided` (`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Localization.Monoidal.Braided:118-123`,
76	    `instance : BraidedCategory (LocalizedMonoidal L W ε)`), which assumes
77	    `[W.IsMonoidal]` and `[BraidedCategory C₀]` (in fact, the project's
78	    `PresheafOfModules` side is `SymmetricCategory`,
79	    `Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Monoidal:145`).
80	  - `[W.IsMonoidal]` for the sheafification `W X` is the project's
81	    `instIsMonoidal_W` at `AlgebraicJacobian/Modules/Monoidal.lean:166-173`,
82	    whose body is a `sorry` for the upstream gap on `stalk_tensorObj`
83	    (varying-ring `R₀`).
84	
85	- **Project's proposed path**: refactor C1's `LineBundle` to depend on
86	  the `CommGroup` of `(Skeleton X.Modules)ˣ`, accepting that it transitively
87	  consumes the deferred `instIsMonoidal_W` sorry.
88	
89	- **Gap**: divergent-with-cost (vs. the strategy text framing
90	  "3 named Mathlib gaps at end-state"). The strategy text's C1 promotion
91	  trigger claims the project's terminal sorry-set is 3 (or 4 with L1802)
92	  named items. After C1 promotion, **every Lean term referencing
93	  `LineBundle X`, `Pic X`, `Pic.pullback`, or `PicardFunctor` would
94	  type-check by drawing on `BraidedCategory (X.Modules)`, hence
95	  transitively on the `instIsMonoidal_W` sorry**. The terminal sorry-set
96	  is unchanged in *count* (the sorry is already in the named set), but the
97	  *transitive reach* of the sorry expands dramatically:
98	    - **Pre-C1 promotion**: `instIsMonoidal_W` is referenced by
99	      `instMonoidalCategoryStruct` / `instMonoidalCategory` (`Modules/Monoidal.lean`)
100	      and nothing else in the project's main proof DAG actually uses
101	      these instances. The sorry is "dormant" in the sense that no
102	      downstream proof obligation explicitly consumes it.
103	    - **Post-C1 promotion**: `instIsMonoidal_W` becomes a *load-bearing*
104	      transitive dependency of `LineBundle`, `Pic`, `Pic.pullback`,
105	      `PicardFunctor`, `PicardFunctorAb`, and the `Jacobian`/`AbelJacobi`
106	      arc whose protected signatures consume `Pic`. The C1 LineBundle
107	      can be *constructed*, but every fact about it that requires the
108	      `CommGroup`/`Monoid` instances is conditional on this sorry.
109	
110	  This expansion is **not dishonest** (the sorry is named and surfaced),
111	  but it is *materially different* from the strategy text's portrayal,
112	  which suggests C1 is "unblocked" by `instIsMonoidal_W`.
113	
114	- **Cost**: the project's bookkeeping language should change. The end-state
115	  framing must say (in `STRATEGY.md`): "the four named Mathlib gaps...
116	  `instIsMonoidal_W` (load-bearing for the C1 Picard group on non-affine
117	  schemes); ..." rather than treating it as cosmetically present.
118	  Additionally, `lean_verify` runs on `Pic.pullback`,
119	  `PicardFunctor`-derived theorems, and `PicardFunctorAb` will surface
120	  `sorryAx` in their axiom chains post-C1, which they do not surface
121	  today.
122	
123	- **Verdict**: **ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB with disclosure cost**. The Mathlib
124	  route requires `BraidedCategory C`; the project's `BraidedCategory
125	  (X.Modules)` is genuinely sorry-conditional. Re-classify
126	  `instIsMonoidal_W` from "dormant deferred gap" to "load-bearing
127	  deferred gap" in the strategy text and in the end-state honest-accounting
128	  paragraph (cf. the C3-JacobianWitness disclosure pattern).
129	
130	### Decision C: pull-back functoriality on `LineBundle`
131	
132	- **Mathlib idiom (analogous to `CommRing.Pic.mapRingHom`)**: a monoidal
133	  functor `F : C ⥤ D` induces a monoid homomorphism `Skeleton C →* Skeleton D`
134	  via `Skeleton.monoidHom` (`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Skeleton:111`),
135	  which restricts to `(Skeleton C)ˣ →* (Skeleton D)ˣ` via `Units.map`.
136	  - The categorical pull-back functor for sheaves of modules is
137	    `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.pullback f : Y.Modules ⥤ X.Modules`
138	    (`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Sheaf:167-168`,
139	    delegating to `SheafOfModules.pullback`).
140	  - To use `Skeleton.monoidHom`, we need a `Functor.Monoidal` instance on
141	    that pullback functor.
142	
143	- **Mathlib status of this instance**: **MISSING**. A search across
144	  `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.*`,
145	  `Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.*`, and
146	  `Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.*` finds no
147	  `Functor.Monoidal` instance for any pullback on `SheafOfModules` /
148	  `PresheafOfModules`. Mathlib *does* have
149	  `(pushforward₀OfCommRingCat F R).Monoidal` for the presheaf-side
150	  pushforward (`...Presheaf/PushforwardZeroMonoidal:33`), but the
151	  pullback-side monoidality is absent.
152	
153	- **Project's proposed path**: C1 refactor would need to derive
154	  `Pic.pullback` from a categorical pull-back functor. Without a
155	  `(SheafOfModules.pullback _).Monoidal` instance, the project must either
156	  (i) build one (a *fifth* Mathlib gap-fill, scope-comparable to building
157	  the `instMonoidalCategory` infrastructure itself), or (ii) define
158	  `Pic.pullback` more concretely — e.g. as `Units.map (Skeleton.monoidHom ...)`
159	  modulo a manual monoidality lemma, or by directly producing
160	  `(Skeleton Y.Modules)ˣ → (Skeleton X.Modules)ˣ` via
161	  `pullback.obj` + ad-hoc μ/ε witnesses.
162	
163	- **Gap**: NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL. **This is the largest hidden cost**
164	  in the C1 promotion as currently scoped. The strategy's "5-8 iters
165	  / 200-300 LOC" estimate for C1 plausibly does not include building
166	  `Functor.Monoidal (X.Hom.pullback f)`, which on its own is
167	  multi-iter / multi-hundred-LOC work analogous to the
168	  `instIsMonoidal_W` chain (modulo that the pullback functor between
169	  sheafifications presents differently from the localization picture
170	  — Mathlib's `SheafOfModules.pullback` is *not* a localization functor).
171	
172	- **Verdict**: **NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL**, with a recommended detour:
173	  define `Pic.pullback` directly via `pullback.obj` on representatives,
174	  manually witnessing `pullback (M ⊗ N) ≅ pullback M ⊗ pullback N` as
175	  the proof obligation. Equivalently, the C1 refactor should produce
176	  this iso as a stand-alone lemma `SheafOfModules.pullback_tensorObj`
177	  (or accept a *second* named deferred sorry as the price of the
178	  refactor).
179	
180	### Decision D: how to register an inhabitant of `LineBundle X`
181	
182	- **Mathlib idiom**: `CommRing.Pic.mk : Type → Pic R`
183	  (`Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup:437-441`) takes a `Module R M`
184	  with a `Module.Invertible R M` typeclass and produces
185	  `Pic R = Shrink (Skeleton (SemimoduleCat R))ˣ` via
186	  `Units.mkOfMulEqOne ⟦.of R M'⟧ ⟦.of R (Dual R M')⟧` plus
187	  a soundness proof.
188	  - The typeclass `Module.Invertible R M` (`...:78`) is defined as
189	    "the canonical map `Mᵛ ⊗[R] M → R` is bijective". This requires the
190	    duality theory of modules — a CommSemiring-level construction.
191	
192	- **Project-side analog requirement**: to register an `M : X.Modules`
193	  as an element of `LineBundle X = (Skeleton X.Modules)ˣ`, the project
194	  must provide a "scheme-side invertibility" witness. Mathlib has no
195	  `SheafOfModules.Invertible` typeclass; the most direct route is to
196	  invoke `Units.mkOfMulEqOne` on `⟦M⟧, ⟦M⁻¹⟧` with a manual proof
197	  `M ⊗ M⁻¹ ≅ 𝟙_` in `X.Modules`.
198	
199	- **Gap**: NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL or PROCEED-by-construction.
200	  - On affine `X = Spec R`: a Mathlib-aligned bridge would derive
201	    `Module.Invertible R M ⇒ Sheaf-side invertibility of `M~``, but the
202	    `~`-functor's monoidal structure (matching presheaf-tensor of
203	    `Tilde` modules with module tensor) is not packaged as a
204	    `Functor.Monoidal` instance in Mathlib b80f227 (and `Tilde` is at
205	    the `ModuleCat` level, not directly at `SheafOfModules`).
206	  - On general `X`: there is no pre-existing typeclass, so each
207	    inhabitant must be hand-rolled with its tensor-inverse witness.
208	
209	- **Verdict**: **PROCEED** for the C1 refactor (i.e. accept that the
210	  framework will not initially ship a typeclass interface for "this
211	  sheaf of modules is invertible"; inhabitants will be `mkOfMulEqOne`-ed
212	  by hand). A future iter (post-C1, outside the project's autonomous
213	  scope) could add `SheafOfModules.Invertible` as the scheme-side
214	  analog of `Module.Invertible`.
215	
216	## Recommendation
217	
218	**The C1 promotion is the mathematically right move and is consistent with
219	Mathlib's idiom**, but the strategy text's portrayal materially understates
220	the costs. Two cost lines are missing from the "5-8 iters / 200-300 LOC"
221	estimate:
222	
223	1. **Disclosure cost**: `instIsMonoidal_W` re-classifies from
224	   "dormant deferred gap" to "load-bearing deferred gap" — every C1+
225	   theorem about `Pic`, `Pic.pullback`, `PicardFunctor`, and the
226	   downstream Jacobian arc transitively depends on it. This is honest
227	   accounting but requires the end-state framing to acknowledge it

10	# Line bundles and the Picard group of a scheme
11	
12	Phase C step C1 (per `STRATEGY.md`, recipe `analogies/c1-route.md`).
13	
14	`LineBundle X` is the units of the (symmetric) monoidal-category skeleton of
15	`X.Modules`, mirroring the ring-side idiom
16	`CommRing.Pic R := Shrink (Skeleton (SemimoduleCat R))ˣ`
17	(`Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup:407`). The `CommGroup` structure derives by

25	(`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Localization.Monoidal.Braided:118`), which needs

29	Jacobian arc transitively consumes that sorry.
30	
31	## Pull-back gap
32	
33	`Pic.pullback` body is `sorry`. It awaits closure of
34	`SheafOfModules.pullback_tensorObj` below — the named-deferred Mathlib gap
35	recording that `Scheme.Modules.pullback f : Y.Modules ⥤ X.Modules` is monoidal.
36	Mathlib b80f227 has no `Functor.Monoidal` instance for this functor; the
37	project takes the named-deferral route per analogist option (c).

48	/-- A line bundle on a scheme `X`: an isomorphism class of invertible
49	quasi-coherent `O_X`-modules, packaged as the units of the skeleton of the
50	symmetric monoidal category `X.Modules`. Mirrors `CommRing.Pic R` which is
51	`Shrink (Skeleton (SemimoduleCat R))ˣ`. -/
52	def LineBundle (X : Scheme.{u}) : Type _ :=
53	  (Skeleton X.Modules)ˣ
54	
55	/-- Tensor product makes the type of line bundles a commutative group: the unit
56	is the structure sheaf `O_X`, multiplication is `⊗_{O_X}`, and the inverse of
57	a line bundle `L` is its dual `L^∨ = Hom_{O_X}(L, O_X)`.
58	
59	Derives via Lean's typeclass resolution from `BraidedCategory (X.Modules)`
60	through `Skeleton.instCommMonoid` and `instCommGroupUnits`. The
61	`BraidedCategory (X.Modules)` instance is provided by
62	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.instBraidedCategory` in
63	`Modules/Monoidal.lean`, transitively depending on `instIsMonoidal_W`. -/
64	noncomputable instance instCommGroupLineBundle (X : Scheme.{u}) :
65	    CommGroup (LineBundle X) :=
66	  inferInstanceAs (CommGroup (Skeleton X.Modules)ˣ)
67	
68	/-- The Picard group of a scheme: line bundles up to isomorphism. -/
69	abbrev Pic (X : Scheme.{u}) : Type _ := LineBundle X
70

73	should be monoidal: `(pullback f).obj (M ⊗ N) ≅ (pullback f).obj M ⊗ (pullback f).obj N`

75	`Functor.Monoidal (Scheme.Modules.pullback f)` instance, so this iso must be
76	hand-built or named-deferred. The project takes the named-deferral route per
77	analogist `c1-route` (`analogies/c1-route.md`).
78	
79	A future Mathlib refresh that lands `(SheafOfModules.pullback _).Monoidal`
80	collapses this sorry to `MonoidalCategoryStruct.tensorObj_iso` (or the
81	canonical successor lemma). -/
82	noncomputable def SheafOfModules.pullback_tensorObj {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ Y)
83	    (M N : Y.Modules) :
84	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj (M ⊗ N) ≅
85	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj M ⊗ (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj N := by
86	  sorry
87	
88	/-- Pull-back of line bundles along a scheme morphism, as a group
89	homomorphism. Eventual implementation is
90	`Units.map (Skeleton.monoidHom (Scheme.Modules.pullback f))` once
91	`Scheme.Modules.pullback f` carries a `Functor.Monoidal` instance — currently a
92	Mathlib gap surfaced as `SheafOfModules.pullback_tensorObj` above. -/
93	noncomputable def Pic.pullback {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ Y) :
94	    Pic Y →* Pic X := by
95	  sorry
96	
97	/-- Pull-back of line bundles along the identity is the identity homomorphism. -/
98	@[simp]
99	lemma Pic.pullback_id (X : Scheme.{u}) : Pic.pullback (𝟙 X) = MonoidHom.id (Pic X) := by
100	  sorry
101	
102	/-- Pull-back of line bundles is contravariantly functorial:
103	`(f ≫ g)^* = f^* ∘ g^*` on Picard groups. -/
104	@[simp]
105	lemma Pic.pullback_comp {X Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) :
106	    Pic.pullback (f ≫ g) = (Pic.pullback f).comp (Pic.pullback g) := by
107	  sorry
